summary:
CONTEXT AND THE APPROACHING STORM: Brandon shares a deeply serious vision received from the Lord while he was engaged in prayer concerning current global conflicts, particularly the war in Ukraine. During this spiritual experience, Brandon witnesses a terrifying and massive storm advancing toward the East Coast of the United States. He describes the storm as impenetrably dark, featuring deep purple hues and violent lightning strikes. As Brandon continues to observe through the spirit, the menacing weather system draws progressively closer until he can clearly see the raindrops falling from the clouds.

THE PROTECTIVE WALL AND THE PEACEFUL SANCTUARY: In stark contrast to the impending threat, Brandon is shown a monumental barrier of protection. He sees a massive wall constructed of gigantic bricks, measuring at least five feet in thickness and soaring over thirty feet into the air, resembling a multi-story building. On Brandon's side of this formidable wall, the environment transforms into a scene of profound beauty and tranquility. Radiant light pierces through the clouds, illuminating a peaceful landscape that features a flowing stream, lush green grass, and a small hut accompanied by a picnic table. Adding to the comforting atmosphere, a chimney stove emits smoke, and the warm, inviting scent of freshly baked bread fills the air.

DIVINE INTERPRETATION OF THE VISION: When Brandon asks God for the meaning behind this vivid imagery, the Lord explains that the scene is a direct representation of Psalm 91 and Psalm 23 operating together. God assures Brandon that those who choose to abide in the secret place of the Most High and remain under the shadow of the Almighty will be completely shielded from the destructive storm. However, this divine protection requires believers to maintain a close relationship with God and to deeply embed the promises of these specific biblical passages into their hearts in preparation for what is coming.

THE NECESSITY OF SPIRITUAL REVELATION OVER MEMORIZATION: A central message of the vision is the critical difference between superficially reciting scripture and possessing true spiritual understanding. Brandon emphasizes that believers cannot simply read or echo verses in a flippant manner. Instead, they must meditate on the words until they become a Rhema word—a deeply internalized, living revelation within their spirit. Without this genuine, internal revelation, Brandon warns that reciting scripture is as powerless as singing a nursery rhyme like Twinkle, Twinkle, Little Star, especially since the devil already knows and understands the scriptures intimately. To effectively wield the word of God as a spiritual sword and achieve real results, particularly when applying verses like James 4:7 to submit to God and resist the devil, the scripture must carry the weight of true revelation knowledge.

FINAL EXHORTATION AND ENCOURAGEMENT: Brandon concludes his message by reiterating the urgent need for spiritual preparedness in the face of forthcoming hardships. He encourages his audience to firmly establish the belief that no weapon formed against them will prosper, ensuring they are mentally and spiritually fortified for the approaching storms. Ending on a hopeful and empowering note, Brandon reminds listeners that the divine presence within them is far greater than any worldly threat, and he bids them a blessed day.
rawData: [June 29, 2023]-[The Lord showed me a massive storm coming on the east coast]-[https://youtu.be/kT2Ztvgdm3s]-[Hi, I have another dream that the Lord wanted me to tell you all about. Now, this one's pretty serious—well, all of them have been serious, but this one was really on my heart this week. It started out as I was praying over the wars and what's going on in Ukraine, and the Lord showed me a massive storm on the East Coast of America. It was as dark as you could see through, like a dark, dark purple storm, and the lightning was striking through it. It was very scary-looking in the natural to look at the storm, and it was getting closer and closer. When I pray, I see in the spirit and the Lord shows me, and the storm gets closer and closer. The last time I saw it, the rain was coming; I could see the raindrops coming out of the storm. But the Lord showed me this massive wall. The wall was probably five feet thick, maybe more, and it was made of humongous bricks; it went up as high as probably thirty feet, or maybe even bigger, like a several-story building. On this side of it, I could see light coming out of it. The light was shining through the clouds and it was beautiful. There was a stream going through on this side of the wall, and there was a little hut—a little house with a picnic table outside of it with green grass—and there was a little stove, a chimney stove with smoke coming out of it, and you could smell the smell of bread. The Lord told me—I said, "Lord, what is this that You're showing me?"—and He said, "Brandon, this is Psalm 91 and Psalm 23, both together." He said, "If you stay in the secret place of the Most High and you abide in that shadow of the Almighty, none of that storm is going to come near you. But you're going to have to stay close to Me and get those scriptures, Psalm 91 and Psalm 23, deep down in your heart. It has to become a Rhema word inside of you for what's coming. You have to know that word, Psalm 91 and Psalm 23. You can't just read, 'The Lord is my shepherd; I shall not want.' You can't just flippantly echo it by just reading it off." You have to meditate on the pages until it becomes a revelation to you and gets deep inside your spirit, so that it is a sword when it comes out of your mouth. In James 4:7, you submit yourself unto God, resist the devil, and he will flee from you. You have to understand that word and get a revelation of it, so that when you are submitting yourself unto God and resisting the devil, he will flee from you. You must have a revelation of it, because if it's not a Rhema word inside of you, you might as well be singing "Twinkle, Twinkle, Little Star," because it's not going to have any power behind it since you're just flippantly saying things. I can quote scripture all day long; the devil knows the scripture, and he understands the scripture better than a lot of Christians do, but it's whenever the word becomes a Rhema to you that it becomes revelation knowledge that comes out of your spirit and becomes powerful. That is what you need to know: whenever that word becomes powerful to you, that's when you're going to get results. That is one of the things the Lord told me to tell you when I was praying this morning, so I'm going to end this video there. I hope you have a blessed day. No weapon formed against you is going to prosper; you have to get that inside of you for when the storms are coming. Greater is he that is in you than he that is in the world. Have a blessed day.]
